meta.title: Regulatory Return — OSHA 1904 Recordability Determination
meta.subtitle: Despatch-bay laceration · reporting year 2025 · US OSHA
meta.author: HSE Operations — Regulatory Returns
meta.classification: Internal working copy — role-labelled; competent-person review required before filing
meta.deid_notice: Internal/shared copy. The injured person and all personnel are referred to by role label; identifiers and health detail were pseudonymised before drafting. The legally-required named submission is held separately and access-controlled; the re-identification key is not part of this document. No injury/illness cell of fewer than 5 is published.

sections.text: Jurisdiction: United States — OSHA 29 CFR 1904 recordkeeping. Return type: OSHA Form 300 log entry (with a Form 301 incident report). Reporting period: calendar year 2025. The recordability test below is applied to the captured incident facts; the determination cites the specific 1904 criterion it applied.

rows.title: Recordable — medical treatment beyond first aid (1904.7(b)(5))
rows.description: Work-related (1904.5), a new case (1904.6); the laceration required sutures — medical treatment beyond first aid (1904.7) — so the case is recordable on the 300 log. A first-aid-only dressing would NOT be recordable; the suture is the deciding fact.
rows.evidence: E-1 occupational-health treatment note (de-identified); E-2 supervisor incident report

metrics.label: 300 entry due
metrics.caption: of learning of the recordable case (1904.29(b)(3))

sections.text: Electronic 300A submission (1904.41) applies if the establishment's size/SIC is in scope — confirm the SIC and employee count for CY2025 before the submission deadline. Never assert a deadline without the jurisdiction.

rows: Laceration to the left forearm — sutured (treatment beyond first aid)
Days-away / restricted: Job transfer or restriction
sections.caption: Working-copy 300 log entry — role-labelled. The legally-required named submission is held separately and access-controlled.

rows: 5 days of restricted work → column classification
sections.caption: Every determination traces to a numbered evidence item.

sections.text: Not applicable to this US return. For an India return this skill runs MANDATORY state detection first and DEFERS to hse-india — factories-act-returns / india-accident-notice / india-state-form-finder. It never mints a national form number; an unverified form value is recorded as [GAP].

Decision-support only. This determination and the prepared return must be reviewed and signed off by a competent person before filing; this is not legal advice. SME review (Regulatory Reporting Specialist) ran and precedes — never replaces — the human competent-person sign-off.
